howItWorks.intro: This housing market correction calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Risk Score based on: Price-to-Income deviation, Inventory levels, and Mortgage rate environment
Equity at Risk = (Home Value × (1 - Correction %)) - Mortgage Balance
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How likely is a housing market correction?
faq.answer: Housing corrections of 10%+ are historically uncommon at the national level but regular at local levels. The 2008 crash saw a 27% national decline. Markets with high price-to-income ratios and rising inventory face higher correction risk. Most analysts distinguish between a correction (10-20% decline) and a crash (20%+).

faq.question: What is a healthy price-to-income ratio?
faq.answer: The historical US average is about 3.5x median household income. Ratios above 5x suggest overvaluation. Many coastal metros now exceed 8-10x, similar to levels seen before the 2008 crash. However, low inventory and structural undersupply can sustain higher ratios longer.

faq.question: Should I sell before a correction?
faq.answer: Timing the market is extremely difficult. If you plan to live in your home long-term (7+ years), short-term declines matter less. If you are heavily leveraged (low equity) in an overvalued market and considering selling anyway, moving sooner reduces downside risk.

faq.question: What protects against a housing correction?
faq.answer: Having at least 20% equity, a fixed-rate mortgage, stable income, and no need to sell soon are the best protections. Underwater homeowners who can continue making payments through a downturn generally recover as markets eventually rebound.

sections.title: What Is the Housing Market Correction Calculator?
sections.body: The Housing Market Correction Calculator helps you assess your investment position with real numbers instead of guesswork. Instead of guessing or spending hours on manual calculations, get accurate results in seconds. Enter your details above and let the calculator do the work.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Market conditions affect your financial security in ways that are hard to quantify without running the actual numbers. This calculator helps you see concrete outcomes based on different scenarios so you can make decisions based on data rather than anxiety or optimism.
